content:
# Acts Chapter 9 — Disagreement Log
**Arbiter:** SOPHIA | **Chapter:** Acts 9 (Saul's Conversion, Ananias, Saul in Damascus/Jerusalem, Peter Heals Aeneas, Peter Raises Tabitha)

| Verse | Issue | EMET | LOGOS | PNEUMA | SOPHIA Resolution |
|-------|-------|------|-------|--------|-------------------|
| 1 | "breathing threat and murder" phrasing | "breathing threat and murder" | "breathing threat and murder" | "breathing out murderous threats" | "breathing threat and murder" -- 2-to-1; preserves the two genitives (threat AND murder) as separate nouns |
| 1 | Opening conjunction | "But Saul" | "But Saul" | "Meanwhile, Saul" | "But Saul" -- Greek de is adversative here, not merely transitional |
| 1 | "went to" vs. "having approached" | "having approached" | "went to" | "went to" | "went to" -- 2-to-1; the aorist participle proselthon functions as a finite verb in narrative |
| 2 | "of the Way" phrasing | "being of the Way" | "who were of the Way" | "belonged to the Way" | "who were of the Way" -- follows LOGOS; all three correctly capitalize "Way" as a proper title |
| 5-6 | "Kick against the goads" (Textus Receptus interpolation) | Omitted (correctly) | Omitted (correctly) | Omitted (correctly) | Omitted -- Sinaiticus, Vaticanus, P74 all lack this material; likely harmonized from Acts 26:14 and 22:10 in later manuscripts |
| 7 | "voice" or "sound" | "the voice" | "the voice" | "the sound of the voice" | "the voice" -- Greek phonen (accusative); PNEUMA adds "sound of" not in the text |
| 8 | Eyes opened paradox | "having-been-opened his eyes, he was-seeing nothing" | "though his eyes were opened, he saw nothing" | "when he opened his eyes, he could not see a thing" | Follows LOGOS; the genitive absolute conveys eyes open yet blind -- PNEUMA's "when he opened" implies volition not in the Greek |
| 10 | "disciple" vs. "follower" | "disciple" | "disciple" | "follower" | "disciple" -- Greek mathetes; SOPHIA retains the traditional rendering throughout |
| 12 | "in a vision" included | Yes (en horamati) | Yes | Yes (integrated) | Yes -- Sinaiticus includes "in a vision" describing Saul's seeing of Ananias; some later manuscripts omit |
| 15 | "chosen vessel" vs. "chosen instrument" | "vessel of-election" | "chosen vessel" | "chosen instrument" | "chosen vessel" -- skeuos means vessel/container; preserves the Hebraic genitive metaphor |
| 15 | "nations" vs. "Gentiles" | "nations" | "Gentiles" | "nations" | "Gentiles" -- ethnon in this context specifically means non-Jewish peoples |
| 17 | Syntax of "Lord ... Jesus" apposition | "the Lord has-sent me, Jesus, the one who-appeared" | "the Lord has sent me -- Jesus, who appeared" | "the Lord Jesus ... has sent me" | Double-dash: "the Lord has sent me -- Jesus, who appeared" -- preserves grammatical roughness of Sinaiticus without PNEUMA's smoothing |
| 18 | "scales" simile | "as scales" | "something like scales" | "something like scales" | "something like scales" -- 2-to-1; hos lepides rendered with the comparative particle preserved |
| 22 | "the Christ" vs. "the Messiah" | "the Christ" | "the Christ" | "the Messiah" | "the Christ" -- 2-to-1; maintains terminological consistency across the translation |
| 25 | "the disciples" vs. "his disciples" | "the disciples" | "his disciples" | "his followers" | "the disciples" -- Sinaiticus lacks autou; SOPHIA follows the manuscript precisely |
| 25 | "basket" type | "basket" | "basket" | "large basket" | "basket" -- PNEUMA adds "large" as interpretation; the Greek spuris does not include a size modifier here |
| 29 | "Hellenists" vs. "Greek-speaking Jews" | "Hellenists" | "Hellenists" | "Greek-speaking Jews" | "Hellenists" -- 2-to-1; the technical term preserves the Greek Hellenistas |
| 31 | "walking in" vs. "living in" | "going in the fear" | "walking in the fear" | "living in reverent awe" | "walking in the fear" -- Greek poreuomene means "walking/going," a metaphor for conduct |
| 33 | Duration phrasing | "from eight years" | "for eight years" | "for eight years" | "for eight years" -- 2-to-1; natural English idiom; EMET's "from" is hyper-literal of Greek ex |
| 34 | "Jesus the Christ" vs. "Jesus Christ" | "Jesus Christ" | "Jesus the Christ" | "Jesus the Messiah" | "Jesus the Christ" -- Sinaiticus has the article (ho Christos), making it a title, not a name component |
| 36 | "acts of mercy" vs. "acts of charity" | "acts-of-mercy" | "acts of charity" | "helping the poor" | "acts of mercy" -- eleemosyne derives from eleos (mercy); more faithful than "charity" or PNEUMA's paraphrase |
| 36 | Tabitha/Dorcas naming | All three preserve both names | All three preserve both names | All three preserve both names | Both Aramaic (Tabitha) and Greek (Dorcas) names preserved with Luke's own translational gloss |
| 38 | "come to us" phrasing | "come-through to us" | "come to us" | "come to us without delay" | "come to us" -- PNEUMA moves "do not delay" into the same clause; SOPHIA follows LOGOS's structure |
| 40 | "Tabitha, arise" vs. "get up" | "Tabitha, rise-up" | "Tabitha, arise" | "Tabitha, get up" | "Tabitha, arise" -- solemn register befits a resurrection command; echoes "Talitha koum" of Mark 5:41 |
| 43 | "tanner" vs. "leatherworker" | "tanner" | "tanner" | "leatherworker" | "tanner" -- 2-to-1; standard translation of burseus; PNEUMA's note on ritual impurity is insightful but does not require a different word |

-- SOPHIA
